title: uxradiogroup
text: uxradiogroup The uxradiogroup widget is a button input control. It allows users to select a single item from a short list of options to make a choice. You can use uxradiogroup in layouts where you want to add a list of options to make a choice and view all options at once. Default Logical Widgets The default logical widgets are: ux-RadioGroup Triggers (web)trigger onChange : This trigger is fired when the value is changed. Extends fluent-radio-group Web Component This widget is implemented using the Fluent web component fluent-radio-group. Most of its features are also available in this widget. Most of its events are mapped to triggers. For more information, see the Fluent documentation on the fluent-radio-group web component. Keyboard support Pressing the Tab key enters the entire radio group. Vertical scrolling with arrow keys to navigate through uxradiogroup options if there is more than one radio button. Pressing the Tab key again exits the radio group. Press Shift + Tab to undo previous Tab key action. Layout processing Widget class method processLayout() constructs the template HTML for this widget. It generates nodes for the control itself as well as slots for label and error icon, and returns it to Uniface. Use this function to enhance the widget with more static features. Widget properties The uxradiogroup widget supports the following properties: Table 1. Widget Properties Property Description html:title Defines a title attribute. html:disabled Defines the widget as being disabled. html:readonly Defines the widget as being read-only. html:tabindex Allows or prevents a field from getting sequential focus usually with the Tab key. html:hidden Defines a boolean value that hides or shows a radiogroup. class:classname Boolean value that adds or removes a specified class. label-text Defines the label text of the radio group widget. Note: This is the label text for the radiogroup and not the individual radio button. Also, the label text as defined by the associated label is not supported. layout Defines the layout of the radio buttons. The available options are: Vertical : A column format where the radio-button and radio-option value is responsive to parent container of the entire radio group with a row-wrap that occurs on the label. Horizontal : A row format where the radio-button and radio-option value is responsive to a max width that displays a tooltip of the text and an ellipsis. After the maximum width of options is met in the row, the entire option with wrap to the following line of the parent container. exclude Defines that there will be no column for the field in the uxdatagrid . Note: This is actually not a widget property. It is a field property, used by the uxdatagrid widget if that is the containing entity's widget. Helper and validation error visualization A uxradiogroup deals with the following error visualizations: Alert icon Tool-tip when hovering the "Alert icon" showing the error message UI blocking When the field is in transaction, the uxradiogroup widget blocks user input by becoming read-only. This avoids race conditions. Widget styling CSS Selector Selected node(s) .u-radio-group The container of widget nodes when end user selects a count of more than one radio button. .u-radio-group.disabled .u-radio-group[disabled] Widget node when the appearance type is disabled. Disable can only occur if the entire radio group is disabled. If one is disabled, then all are disabled. .u-radio-group:active Widget node when the widget is active. .u-radio-group:focus Widget node when the widget has focus. This applies to tabular-focus from keyboard navigation. .u-radio Radio button nodes. .u-radio:first-child .u-radio:nth-child(n) .u-radio:last-child A single radio button node based on its index. .u-radio.rest .u-radio:nth-child(n).rest The group of radio button nodes when in rest. CSS child selectors can be used to select specific radio buttons based on their index. .u-radio:hover .u-radio:nth-child(n) :hover A radio button node when the mouse enters over the container. Appearance type is Hover . When mouse enters a new container, the previous hovered container returns to rest. .u-radio.pressed .u-radio:nth-child(n):pressed A radio button node when the mouse clicks the container. Appearance type is Pressed . Pressed state persists until another click on a different container occurs in the radio group. If a different radio button is clicked, remove the pressed state and apply to a new selection. Widget sources The widget source files, such as JavaScript and CSS files, can be downloaded from the following GitHub repository: https://github.com/RocketSoftware/uniface-ux/tree/main/src/ux/radio_group . Related Links ux-RadioGroup This section provides description for the logical UX widget ux-RadioGroup properties. UX Widgets Uniface UX provides a set of widgets that are implemented using the UX Widget Interface. This set is referred as UX Widgets. The sources of these widgets are available and allow changes and/or extensions to the widgets. In a default Uniface installation, you can find the sources in the <install>\uniface\webapps\uniface\common\ux folder. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
